shot: 03 — Before forests. Before apples. Before squirrels had anywhere to hide nuts.
rationale: Much more visually unique than the ocean/barren shark frame: dark tabletop museum-stage gag instead of landscape.
Contains readable apple, squirrel(s), and acorn/nut forms without generated text.
Avoids the large tree/forest failure that appeared in the first v12 outdoor pass.
Holds the say-dog-see-dog idea better than more abstract v12c variants.
caveat: Not final-lock quality: v12b05 has duplicate squirrels and the acorns could be cleaner/larger. If this becomes a hero gag shot, do one more pass or manually composite the three props for perfect readability.
failed_lessons: Outdoor/scenic prompt cues immediately caused forests/branches/leaves even with negative prompts.
Black-stage prompt prevents scenery drift but the model starts turning the acorn into abstract display objects; guide/control needs to be simpler or composited.
